source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Recognise the place value of each digit in a three-digit number; compare and order numbers up to 1000; and find 10 or 100 more or less than a given number.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack connects quantities, base-ten structure, numerals, words and number-line positions so learners reason about three-digit numbers rather than reading digits independently.

objective.child_goal: I can build, read, compare and change three-digit numbers using hundreds, tens and ones.
objective.statement: Represent and partition numbers to 1000, identify each digit's value, compare and order numbers, and find 10 or 100 more or less.
misconceptions: Reads a digit by its face value and ignores its position, for example saying the 4 in 472 is worth 4.
Treats zero as removable and reads 406 as 46 or builds it with four hundreds and six tens.
Compares numbers by a later digit or by numeral length without comparing hundreds, then tens, then ones.
Changes the wrong place or changes several places when finding 10 or 100 more or less.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The value a digit has because of where it is in a number.
vocabulary.audio_script: A digit's place tells us whether it is worth hundreds, tens or ones.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A digit card linked to a labelled hundreds, tens and ones column.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Split a number into useful parts without changing its total.
vocabulary.audio_script: Partition means split a number into parts that still combine to make the same whole.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A zero that keeps another digit in the correct place.
vocabulary.audio_script: In 406, zero shows there are no tens and keeps the 6 in the ones place.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: An empty tens column held open by a zero card.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Decide which number is greater, smaller or equal.
vocabulary.audio_script: Compare the hundreds first. If they match, compare the tens, then the ones.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Two place-value charts checked from left to right.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Bundle ten tens. What new unit have you made, and what value stayed the same?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Reactivate the base-ten exchange that links two-digit understanding to hundreds.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Ten tens have the same value as one hundred. The arrangement changes, but the total does not.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Ten ten-rods sit beside one hundred-square with an equality link.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build 374 crates using hundreds, tens and ones, then match the build to the numeral.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce three-digit numerals as records of grouped quantities.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Three hundreds, seven tens and four ones make three hundred and seventy-four.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Explorer supply crates group into hundred pallets, ten-crate rows and single crates beside an HTO chart.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build 406. There are four hundreds, no tens and six ones, so the zero must remain in the tens place.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model zero as a placeholder and connect empty quantity columns to written notation.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The tens column is empty, but the zero keeps the hundreds and ones in their correct places.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Four hundred-squares and six ones align with 4, 0 and 6 in a place-value chart.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build the number, write its expanded form, then show one different valid partition.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Coordinate concrete, pictorial and symbolic representations while introducing flexible partitioning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: First use standard parts. Then exchange one hundred or one ten and check that the total is unchanged.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A base-ten workspace links to numeral, words, expanded notation and a total counter.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which is greater, 509 or 590? Then find 10 less than the greater number.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect digit-by-digit comparison errors and insecure changes across tens.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The hundreds match, so compare the tens. To find ten less, change the tens value while tracking any exchange.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Two HTO charts are inspected left to right, then the selected number moves one ten-step on a number line.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach your explorer partner why 398 becomes 408 when 10 is added.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Gather explanation evidence for exchange across a hundred boundary.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Show what happens to the tens and hundreds, and explain why the ones stay the same.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Base-ten blocks, an HTO chart and a marked number line remain visible for the learner's explanation.

manipulatives.purpose: Build, exchange and partition three-digit quantities while preserving the total.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners add hundred-squares, ten-rods and ones, exchange ten equal units, or use labelled stepper buttons; the linked numeral and expanded form update only after the learner confirms each place.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Every unit has text, shape and spoken value cues; keyboard commands and single-tap steppers replace dragging; a live region announces each column and total; high-contrast outlines, undo, and a show-one-place mode support low vision, motor access and working memory.

manipulatives.purpose: Connect relative magnitude, left-to-right comparison and 10/100 changes to number-line movement.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners compare linked HTO charts, place markers on calibrated lines and move by labelled jumps of 10 or 100.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Markers can be positioned by number entry, arrow keys or choice buttons; tick labels can be increased; colour is redundant; and reduced-motion mode uses before/after positions with a spoken jump description.

practice.interleaving_notes: Secure standard partitioning and placeholder zero before mixing flexible partitions, comparison, ordering and 10/100 changes across boundaries.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Connect grouped quantities, spoken number names and three-digit numerals.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: number 100-999 + base-ten build or numeral target + HTO labels + optional number-word match
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Balance all digit positions, include teen tens carefully in spoken forms and avoid relying on colour to distinguish units.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Interpret and construct numbers containing zero and record standard expanded form.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: number with zero in tens or ones + empty quantity column + numeral/expanded-form choices + close shifted-digit distractors
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include 3-digit multiples of 10 and numbers such as 304, 470 and 900; narration must explicitly name empty places.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Compare, order and locate numbers to 1000 using place-value evidence.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: two to five three-digit numbers + HTO comparison path + calibrated or partially labelled number line
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use close pairs with equal hundreds or tens; require a stated comparison reason rather than symbol recognition alone.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Find 10 or 100 more or less, including changes across a tens or hundreds boundary.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: start number + signed jump of 10 or 100 + base-ten exchange path + HTO before/after + explanation stem
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Check all generated values remain between 0 and 1000; track which places change and accept equivalent exchange explanations.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain representation matching, comparison and place changes across spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue weighted to prior misconception + one representation switch + one concise explain-or-check prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep daily retrieval short, provide audio number names and avoid speed scoring.

body.prompt: Build 374 using hundreds, tens and ones.

hints: Read the digit in each labelled column.
The 7 is in the tens place, so it is worth 70.
question_variants.explanation: 374 is 3 hundreds, 7 tens and 4 ones, which is 300 + 70 + 4.

body.prompt: Which expanded form matches 406?

hints: The 4 is in the hundreds place.
Zero tells us there are no tens.
question_variants.explanation: 406 has 4 hundreds, 0 tens and 6 ones, so its value is 400 + 6.

body.prompt: Choose the greater number and explain which place decides it.

hints: Both numbers have 5 hundreds.
Compare 0 tens with 9 tens.
question_variants.explanation: The hundreds are equal, so compare the tens. Nine tens is greater than zero tens, so 590 is greater than 509.

body.prompt: Find 10 more than 398 and choose the places that change.

hints: Add one ten to 9 tens.
Ten tens exchange for one hundred; the 8 ones stay the same.
question_variants.explanation: 398 plus 10 is 408. The 9 tens become 10 tens, which exchange for 1 hundred, so the hundreds and tens digits change.

body.prompt: Build 123 using hundreds, tens and ones at the island supply camp.
body.keyboard_instructions: Use H, T and O stepper controls, then Enter to check.

hints: Start with 1 hundred.
Then add 2 tens and 3 ones.
question_variants.explanation: 123 is 1 hundreds, 2 tens and 3 ones: 100 + 20 + 3.

body.prompt: The supply counter shows 123. Which expanded form decomposes it correctly?
body.keyboard_instructions: Use H, T and O stepper controls, then Enter to check.

hints: Read the hundreds, tens and ones columns separately.
The digit 2 in the tens place is worth 20.
question_variants.explanation: 123 decomposes as 100 + 20 + 3. Each digit keeps the value of its place.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Reads a digit by its face value and ignores its position, for example saying the 4 in 472 is worth 4.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner names a selected digit without its unit or builds four ones for the hundreds digit.
repair_sequence: Place the digit in a labelled HTO chart.
Build the matching quantity in that one column.
Say digit, place and value together: 4, hundreds, 400.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner identifies the value of each digit in a new three-digit number and matches it to quantity.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats zero as removable and reads 406 as 46 or builds it with four hundreds and six tens.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner omits the empty tens column or shifts the final digit left.
repair_sequence: Build four hundreds and six ones with the tens column visibly empty.
Insert a zero card to record the empty tens place.
Compare 406 and 46 side by side using quantities and number lines.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner builds and writes a new number containing zero without shifting digits.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Compares numbers by a later digit or by numeral length without comparing hundreds, then tens, then ones.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner says 509 is greater than 590 because 9 is greater than 0 or checks the ones before the tens.
repair_sequence: Align both numbers in HTO charts.
Compare matching columns from hundreds to ones and stop at the first difference.
Confirm relative position on a number line.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner compares a close pair and names the first place that decides the order.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Changes the wrong place or changes several places when finding 10 or 100 more or less.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner adds 1 to every digit, changes the ones for a ten-jump, or misses an exchange at a boundary.
repair_sequence: Build the starting number and add or remove one ten or one hundred unit.
Exchange only when a column reaches ten or needs renaming.
Record the before-and-after HTO charts and circle changed places.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner completes two new 10/100 changes, including one boundary case, and explains which places changed.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a plain HTO chart, muted island-map background, one highlighted place at a time and no particle celebrations.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace moving base-ten exchanges and number-line jumps with labelled before/after panels controlled by a Next button.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read every number in full, then announce digit, place and value; provide separate replay for the prompt and each representation.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Keep instructions to one action per sentence, pair words with H/T/O labels and leave quantity models visible while symbols are interpreted.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Use a consistent build-read-partition-check routine, hide inactive controls and show a short progress strip.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Remove timers, allow unlimited exchanges and undo, and offer side-by-side rather than transient comparisons.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Preserve correctly built places during repair and describe a wrong answer as a place-value clue to inspect.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach hundred, ten, one, digit, value, greater, less and equal with matched blocks, gestures and spoken examples.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires at least 80% across building, reading, standard partitioning and comparison, including numbers with zero. Secure requires 90% across close comparisons, flexible partitions and 10/100 changes including boundaries on two retained sessions.
evidence.parent_summary: Can build and explain three-digit numbers, compare them and work out 10 or 100 more or less.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Stores quantity build, digit values, placeholder use, partition, comparison reason and changed places separately so a correct numeral does not hide insecure place-value reasoning.
next_actions: Route shifted-zero errors to the empty-column placeholder repair.
Use secure place-value knowledge in three-digit addition and subtraction.
Interleave number-line estimation and ordering after representation matching is stable.
Schedule mixed retrieval at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 3 place-value pack with four preserved curated variants and 216 deterministic pilot candidates. The bank covers composition, decomposition, zero placeholders, flexible exchanges, comparison, ordering, calibrated number lines, estimation and 10-or-100 changes through renderer-supported formats. Generated candidates remain in review until curriculum, teacher, accessibility and safeguarding checks are complete.
